voice.tagline: What does this mean, and where is the opportunity?
not: over-explaining context the audience already knows
yes: original synthesis connecting dots others miss
evidence before argument - show the story, then name the structure
implications for African founders and builders
structural market analysis with a clear so-what
voice.pov: Third person only. No 'we' or 'you' in editorial content.
voice.tone: Direct. Dense. No filler. Every sentence earns its place.
opener_rules: Scene-setting or strong claim in the first sentence
Opener must work as a standalone hook - reader should want the next sentence

audience.primary: African founders, builders, PMs, and early-stage investors
audience.persona_summary: Riara, 31, Nairobi PM at a Series A startup. Reading on Tecno on the morning commute. Will abandon if the opener is context she already knows. Does not read listicles. Reads essays.
audience.core_question: Am I smarter after reading this? Did this give me an edge?

frontier-reports.description: Deep research. Structural market analysis. Long-form explanation of what most people have wrong.
frontier-reports.test: Does this explain something structural that most people have wrong?

market-signals.description: Shorter synthesis. Hidden trends. What data reveals that most people are reading wrong.
market-signals.test: Is there a signal here most people are misreading?

build-opportunities.description: Problems worth solving. Market openings created by the signals. Startup ideas derived from research.
build-opportunities.test: Is there a clear product or business opportunity from this signal?

article_structure.section_dividers: Use --- between sections. Never use ## headers in article body.
article_structure.closer: Ends on an implication for African builders. Forward-looking read. Not a summary.

linkedin.format_type: Long-form TEXT POST — not a LinkedIn Article
linkedin.hook_rule: First 2 lines must work standalone as the hook (visible before see more)
rules: No section headers (## or bold headers)
Ends with a question or observation that invites a response
linkedin.algorithm_note: LinkedIn suppresses Articles. Text posts get distribution. This distinction is non-negotiable.

structure: Tweet 2: Context - what most people think
Tweet 3: Insight - what the data shows
Tweet 4: Opportunity - what this means for builders
Tweet 5: CTA - follow TechTribe Africa for more

lint_rules.contractions: None allowed. Write out in full: did not, it is, they are, cannot, would not.
lint_rules.credential_safety: No API keys, tokens, or secrets in any content file.

whatsapp_as_operating_layer.thesis: WhatsApp is not a channel in the African business stack. It is the stack.
open_angles: WhatsApp Business API competitive landscape in 2026
Local BSP alternatives and their unit economics
WhatsApp as customer data layer and what it means for AI

memory_crm_gap.thesis: African businesses run on memory, not CRM. The gap is not a feature gap - it is a trust and workflow gap.
open_angles: Specific verticals where memory failure is most expensive (healthcare, legal, logistics)
What retention looks like for the first follow-up SaaS that cracks this

missing_middle_of_software.thesis: Africa's software gap is not a volume problem. It is a pricing and workflow mismatch between spreadsheets and enterprise tools.
open_angles: Pricing model experiments that are working: pay-per-use vs subscription
Which African SaaS companies crossed the $50 barrier and how
The missing middle in specific verticals: logistics, healthcare, education

mobile_money_infrastructure.thesis: Mobile money in Africa is not a fintech product. It is infrastructure - with all the economics, fragility, and opportunity that implies.
open_angles: Cross-border mobile money rails: what works, what does not, who wins
Float as a balance sheet risk: how agent networks manage it at scale
The super-agent model emerging in East Africa

fintech_regulation.thesis: Fintech licensing in Africa is not a compliance checkbox. It is an architecture decision that shapes product, pricing, and market.
open_angles: Kenya vs Nigeria vs Ghana licensing comparison in 2026
The sandbox pathway and who is using it
Regulatory arbitrage as a startup strategy: when it works and when it collapses

digital_infrastructure.thesis: Africa's digital infrastructure gap is closing faster than the investment narrative priced in.
open_angles: Data centre economics: when local hosting becomes cheaper than AWS
Power reliability as a product constraint and how builders design around it
The telco API access bottleneck beyond WhatsApp

startup_ecosystem.thesis: African startup funding dynamics are not a cyclical slowdown. They are a structural reset driven by LP exits and bridge round mechanics.
open_angles: Which markets are attracting capital in 2026 and which are being abandoned
The Series A gap: why the bridge round problem compounds at scale
Acqui-hire economics: what incumbents are actually paying and why

ai_in_africa.thesis: AI adoption in Africa is not a hype story. It is a cost-constraint story. The tools African founders use are not the tools being sold to them.
open_angles: Local language model gaps and what they mean for product builders
AI agent use cases that work inside African business workflows
The open-source vs proprietary trade-off for African startups on tight compute budgets

stablecoin_and_crypto.thesis: Africa leads the world in stablecoin adoption because it solves a real problem: cross-border value transfer without correspondent banking friction.
open_angles: Stablecoin rails for B2B trade finance in African cross-border commerce
CBDC rollouts vs stablecoin adoption: who is winning and why
The on-ramp problem: converting local currency to stablecoin at scale
